body: PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) recorded a net foreign transaction of 330.10 million shares, with foreign buys reaching IDR 1.68 billion and foreign sells reaching IDR 1.35 billion, despite its stock price weakening 2.86% to IDR 68 per share. PT Daya Intiguna Yasa Tbk (MDIY), a retail company that debuted on the stock market, came in second with a net foreign transaction of 109.44 million shares, with its stock price closing at IDR 1,690 per share.
title: Goto Gojek Tokopedia surpasses Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur in net foreign transactions on Indonesian stock market.

body: PT Habco Trans Maritima Tbk (HATM) is carrying out a rights issue of 1.8 billion shares at a nominal value of IDR 50 per share, which will increase the total shares issued by 25.71% to 7 billion shares. The transaction value is estimated to be IDR 457.20 billion, assuming a share price of IDR 254 per share, with the controlling shareholders holding 82.27% of the shares and the remaining 17.73% held by non-controlling shareholders.
title: PT Habco Trans Maritima to issue 1.8 billion new shares in rights issue, increasing total shares by 25.71%.

body: The Indonesian government, together with PT Perusahaan Listrik Negara (PLN), aims to achieve a new renewable energy target of 17.87% and plans to set a more ambitious target of 60% by 2050 and 70% by 2060, with an estimated investment of $14.2 billion or IDR 22.78 trillion by 2025. The government will prepare an electricity supply business plan for 2025-2035 with PLN and independent power producers to meet these targets, which is expected to support economic growth of up to 8% targeted by President Prabowo Subianto.
title: Indonesian Government and PLN Collaborate to Achieve 17.87% Renewable Energy Target by 2025

body: The Jakarta Composite Index (JCI) dropped 0.74% on Thursday, driven by the unsatisfactory performance of several large-cap stocks, including PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA), PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk (BREN), PT Bayan Resources Tbk (BYAN), PT Amman Mineral Internasional Tbk (AMMN), and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI), which saw their market capitalization decline. The stocks' performance varied, with BBCA down 3.9%, BREN up 2.32%, BYAN down 2.17%, AMMN down 5.89%, and BBRI down 4% compared to their prices last week.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Falls 0.74% as Large-Cap Stocks Weaken

body: PT Daya Intiguna Yasa Tbk (MDIY), the issuer behind the Mr. D.I.Y retail brand, is poised to capitalize on the substantial non-grocery retail segment in Indonesia, which has a total addressable market of $18.4 billion and is forecasted to grow at an 8% compound annual growth rate from 2023 to 2028. MDIY has already increased its market penetration to 1.9% in 2023 and aims to further expand its market share through efficient operational strategies, having achieved a 109% revenue growth from 2021 to 2023, with total revenue of IDR 3.9 trillion and net profit of IDR 161.12 billion in 2023.
title: PT Daya Intiguna Yasa Tbk, owner of Mr. D.I.Y retail brand, eyes growth in non-grocery retail segment with IPO listing.

body: PT Daya Inti Yasa Tbk (MDIY) raised IDR 4.15 trillion from its initial public offering (IPO) on the Indonesia Stock Exchange, with the IPO price set at IDR 1,650 per share, but saw a 14.58% decline to IDR 1,405 per share shortly after opening, before recovering to IDR 1,820 per share. The IPO proceeds will be allocated towards strategic requirements, including 60% for bank loan settlement, 30% for outlet expansion, and 10% for working capital, as MDIY aims to strengthen its market position and expand its outlet network across Indonesia.
title: PT Daya Inti Guna Yasa Tbk makes debut on Indonesia Stock Exchange with IDR 4.15 trillion IPO proceeds.

body: PT Hero Global Investment Tbk (HGII) is set to raise up to IDR 299 billion through its initial public offering of 1.3 billion shares, priced between IDR 200 and IDR 230 per share, with 66% of the proceeds allocated to its subsidiary PT Siantar Sitanduk Energi for a hydroelectric power plant project in North Sumatra. The remaining funds will be used for capital injection into PT Multiprima Hidro Energi for a minihydro power plant project and working capital, with PT OCBC Sekuritas Indonesia and PT UOB Kay Hian Sekuritas appointed as underwriters.
title: PT Hero Global Investment to Raise Up to IDR 299 Billion in IPO for Renewable Energy Projects.

body: Bank Indonesia will increase its macroprudential liquidity incentive policy assistance to IDR 290 trillion, a 15.54% rise from IDR 251 trillion this year, with state-owned banks receiving IDR 126 trillion, private banks getting IDR 129 trillion, and regional development banks receiving IDR 30 trillion. The additional liquidity of IDR 39 trillion will focus on sectors that generate the most employment, including agriculture, trade, housing, and manufacturing industries.
title: Bank Indonesia to Increase Macroprudential Liquidity Incentive Policy Assistance to IDR 290 Trillion for Hundreds of Banks Starting Next Year.

body: PT Energi Mega Persada Tbk (ENRG) plans to conduct a share buyback with a budget of USD 12 million, equivalent to approximately IDR 192.23 billion, to enhance shareholder value and reduce outstanding shares, with the number of shares to be repurchased not exceeding 10% of the company's issued and paid-up capital. The share buyback is expected to be carried out over a maximum period of 12 months following shareholder approval, which will be sought at a meeting scheduled for January 24, 2025.
title: Energia Mega Persada to Conduct Share Buyback Worth USD 12 Million to Enhance Shareholder Value.

body: PT Merdeka Copper Gold Tbk (MDKA) reported a 42.5% year-over-year revenue growth to USD 1.67 billion in the first nine months of 2024, but its net loss widened to USD 67.02 million due to increased costs, including a 47.4% surge in cost of goods sold and higher financial expenses. The company's share price fell 4.35% to IDR 1,760 per share, marking a 34.57% decline since the beginning of the year.
title: PT Merdeka Copper Gold's revenue grows 42.5% but net loss widens to USD 67.02 million due to increased expenses.

body: The Jakarta Composite Index (JCI) fell 0.7% to 7,107.88, its lowest point since the market opened, with a total transaction value of IDR 11.05 trillion, 6.4% lower than the previous day. Foreign investors posted a net foreign sell of IDR 437.9 billion, with top gainers including PT Barito Renewables Energi Tbk (up 4.45%) and PT Krida Jaringan Nusantara Tbk (up 34.55%), while top losers included PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (down 2.35%) and PT Satria Antaran Prima Tbk (down 25%).
title: Jakarta Composite Index Falls 0.7% to Lowest Point Since Market Opened

body: Foreign investors in Indonesia's stock market recorded a net sell of IDR 473.97 billion (USD 29.44 million) on Wednesday, with the year-to-date net foreign value still in the positive territory at IDR 17.20 trillion (USD 1.06 billion). The top purchases were PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (ENERGY) with 132.78 million shares, PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BUMI) with 57.10 million shares, and PT MNC Kapital Tbk (BHIT) with 15.69 million shares, while the top sales were PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) with -386.47 million shares and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) with -64.04 million shares.
title: Foreign investors in Indonesia's stock market record net sell of IDR 473.97 billion, or USD 29.44 million, on Wednesday.

body: PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (BIPI) recorded a net foreign buy of 129.48 million shares, with a transaction volume of 75.03 million shares worth IDR 15.81 billion, and its share price closed at IDR 90 per share, up IDR 1 from the previous day. PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) followed with a net foreign buy of 57.10 million shares, with a transaction volume of 426.36 million shares worth IDR 161.15 billion, and its share price closed at IDR 376 per share, down IDR 4 from the previous day.
title: PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Leads Net Foreign Buying on Indonesia Stock Exchange

body: Barokah Melayu Food Pte Ltd (BMF) will increase its stake in PT Champ Resto Indonesia Tbk from 42.46% to 50.10% through a voluntary tender, acquiring 165.50 million shares at IDR 1,025 per share, totaling IDR 169.63 billion. After the transaction, BMF's stake will rise to 50.10%, followed by Ali Gunawan Budiman with 14.40%, PT Alba Cipta Rasa with 13.62%, PT Cipta Rasa Juara with 7.60%, and the public with 14.27%.
title: Barokah Melayu Food to increase stake in PT Champ Resto Indonesia through voluntary tender.

body: Bank Indonesia (BI) has maintained its BI rate at 6%, deposit facility rate at 5.25%, and lending facility at 6.75% to reinforce stability in the rupiah exchange rate amid immense uncertainty in the global economy. The rupiah exchange rate weakened 1.37% in December 2024, and BI will optimize its monetary instruments to attract short-term portfolio investments and bolster the rupiah.
title: Bank Indonesia holds interest rates steady to stabilize rupiah exchange rate amid global economic uncertainty.

body: PT Bukit Asam Tbk (PTBA) is developing a pilot project to transform low-calorie coals into humic acid, a high-value product, after launching a prototype at IUP Peranap, Riau, in collaboration with Universitas Gadjah Mada. The project aims to generate added value and support a sustainable economy, with potential economic benefits, as humic acid is an organic substance that improves land fertility and structure.
title: PT Bukit Asam to Develop Pilot Project to Transform Low-Calorie Coals into Humic Acid

body: Foreign investors have registered a net outflow for 4 consecutive days, with a total of IDR 6.83 trillion (approximately USD 460 million) in net foreign sells, led by PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) with IDR 666.5 billion, PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) with IDR 407.8 billion, and PT Merdeka Copper Gold Tbk (MDKA) with IDR 145.5 billion. The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) has been in a downtrend, correcting 1.39% to 7,157.73, in line with the foreign outflow trend.
title: Foreign Investors Register Net Outflow for 4 Consecutive Days Amid Jakarta Stock Price Correction

body: PT Industri Jamu dan Farmasi Sido Muncul Tbk (SIDO) plans to allocate IDR 150-175 billion in capital expenditure next year, focusing on production facility maintenance and technology development to support digitalization and marketing, aiming for low double-digit growth. The company targets a minimum 10% growth in revenue and net profit, driven by expanded distribution and new product introductions targeting Gen Z and millennials, with a strong track record of 32.65% year-on-year profit growth and a high net profit margin of 29.62%.
title: Sido Muncul Allocates IDR 150-175 Billion for Capital Expenditure to Support Digitalization and Growth.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index fell 1.39% or 100.9 points to 7,157.73, marking its fourth consecutive day of decline, with banking companies such as PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk, PT Bank Central Asia Tbk, and PT Bank Mandiri Tbk leading the losses. Meanwhile, PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk was the top gainer, boosting the index by 13.96 points, while 11 sectoral indices also declined, with the industry sector experiencing the largest drop of 2.32%.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Falls 1.39% to 7,157.73, Led by Banking Sector Decline

body: Foreign investors sold a net 991.73 million shares on Tuesday, December 17, with a total value of IDR 1.63 trillion (USD 101.62 million), led by sales of PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) shares, PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) shares, and PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) shares. Despite this, year-to-date net foreign value remains in the green zone at IDR 17.67 trillion (USD 1.10 billion), with notable buys including PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (BIPI) shares and PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) shares.
title: Foreign investors in Indonesia's stock market record net sell of IDR 1.63 trillion, or USD 101.62 million, on Tuesday, December 17.

body: PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur TBK (no ticker mentioned) took the top spot in the net foreign list, with a net foreign buy of 155.99 million shares, while PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK (GOTO) was driven out of the top 10 list with a net foreign of 3.23 billion shares. PT Janu Putra Sejahtera TBK (no ticker mentioned) came in second, with a net foreign of 32.82 million shares, and PT Bipi's (BIPI) stock price closed at IDR 89, up from IDR 88 per share earlier in the day.
title: PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Takes Top Spot in Net Foreign Buying List
